pr.title: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched one sysdolphin synth function and three Ice Mountain stage functions. The PR removed an obsolete residual-match note for HSD_Synth_80388DC8, corrected the HSD_Synth_8038B120 prototype to void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void), and reshaped gricemt.c source for grIceMt_801F96E0, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA0BC until GALE01 reported all four targeted functions at 100.0%.
what_changed: Added `#pragma auto_inline off` before `grIceMt_801F71E8`, affecting inlining behavior used by later Ice Mountain matching.
Replaced some `HSD_ASSERT` and `HSD_ASSERTMSG` macro uses in `grIceMt_801F96E0` and `grIceMt_801F993C` with explicit `if` checks calling `__assert` using string-table offsets from `grIm_803E4068`.

Reshaped `grIceMt_801FA0BC`: renamed locals, removed the previous `grIceMt_801F71E8_inner` / `grIceMt_801F71E8_noinline` inline-wrapper tactic, added `PAD_STACK(24)`, and introduced a small inline helper `grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs(Ground* g)` returning `&g->gv.icemt2.xC8`.
Changed `grIceMt_801FA0BC` translation math from a literal `-20.0f * Ground_801C0498()` form to `grIm_804DB570 * Ground_801C0498()`, preserving source shape needed for matching.
In `grIceMt_801FA0BC`, changed null checks to explicit `== NULL` / `!= NULL` forms for gobj, jobj, and jobjs entries, and scoped the `jobjs` temporary around flag handling.
Removed the stale todo comment in `synth.c` stating `HSD_Synth_80388DC8` was only a 97.2% match.
Corrected the `synth.h` declaration of `HSD_Synth_8038B120` from `UNK_RET HSD_Synth_8038B120(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void)`.
smart_moves: Used explicit `__assert` calls with file/message string pointers instead of assertion macros where macro expansion did not produce the target codegen.
Controlled inlining globally near `grIceMt_801F71E8` with `#pragma auto_inline off` rather than relying on extra wrapper functions that were removed in the final source shape.
Introduced a tiny accessor helper, `grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs`, to influence pointer expression/codegen for the `g->gv.icemt2.xC8` JObj array without adding broad structural changes.
Matched float expression/codegen in `grIceMt_801FA0BC` by using the existing global `grIm_804DB570` instead of an equivalent literal expression.
Cleaned up header truth while matching: the synth function prototype was tightened to the known `void (void)` signature instead of leaving generic `UNK_RET` / `UNK_PARAMS` placeholders.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn stage matches, source-shape changes around asserts, temporary lifetimes, block scope, and explicit null comparisons can be decisive even when behavior is unchanged.
Do not keep obsolete partial-match comments after a function reaches 100%; stale todo notes reduce search quality and mislead future work.

Header prototypes should be narrowed when evidence is sufficient. Replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with a concrete `void HSD_Synth_8038B120(void)` improves type information and require-protos compatibility.
Equivalent constants are not always equivalent for matching: a named data symbol such as `grIm_804DB570` may be needed instead of a literal like `-20.0f`.
Small inline helpers that return interior struct pointers can be a useful matching tactic when direct field access produces different register allocation or instruction shape.
naming_conventions: Target Ice Mountain functions retain the `grIceMt_` prefix, e.g. `grIceMt_801F96E0`, `grIceMt_801F993C`, and `grIceMt_801FA0BC`.
The newly added helper name `grIceMt_FA0BC_jobjs` ties the helper to the parent function address suffix and describes that it returns JObj pointers.
Sysdolphin synth unknown/address-based names remain in the `HSD_Synth_8038xxxx` style until better semantic names are known.
Local names in the matched source moved toward concise semantic forms such as `gobj`, `jobj`, `gp`, `frame`, and `jobjs`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma auto_inline off` was applied before `grIceMt_801F71E8` to manage inlining-sensitive codegen.
Assertion macros were expanded manually into `if` guards plus `__assert` calls to match emitted code and exact assert string operands.
`PAD_STACK(24)` was added in `grIceMt_801FA0BC`, indicating stack layout/padding was part of the match.
A helper returning `&g->gv.icemt2.xC8` was used to shape pointer handling for a four-entry JObj array.
Explicit `!= NULL` checks were used before `Ground_801C2D0C` calls for `jobjs[0]` through `jobjs[3]`.
Using `grIm_804DB570 * Ground_801C0498()` instead of a direct `-20.0f` literal was part of the final matching source shape.
Verification listed `python configure.py --require-protos`, `ninja`, and GALE01 reports showing `HSD_Synth_80388DC8`, `grIceMt_801F96E0`, `grIceMt_801F993C`, and `grIceMt_801FA0BC` at 100.0%.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence is the PR description, which documents the matched functions, prototype cleanup, and verification commands.
searchable_terms: Match synth and Ice Mountain functions
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that name or type `grIceMt_GroundVars`, `icemt2.xC4`, and `icemt2.xC8` more precisely.
Search for other stage files where replacing `HSD_ASSERT` macros with explicit `__assert` calls helped matching.
Search for other uses of `#pragma auto_inline off` around helper lookup functions in stage code.
Check whether `grIm_804DB570` is later named as a float constant for Ice Mountain vertical movement.
Find future synth PRs that further rename `HSD_Synth_80388DC8` or `HSD_Synth_8038B120`.
